theme: Polish what's there — the office looks alive, now make it feel intentional
visual_observations: Desktop: Office view is cohesive dark theme, Bloberto at manager desk with CVO title, 6 team desks populated. Luna appears at TWO desks (row 1 working + row 2 ghost) — the dual-render bug persists because workers.json entries lacked 'id' field causing activeIds matching to fail. Dashboard: Clean stats cards, Luna shows as active with progress bar. Whiteboard is properly dark-themed now. Mobile: Layout works but character names at desks are hard to read. Coffee machine, plants, and decorations add life. Overall vibe badge 'Crushing It' with fire emoji works well.

ideas.title: Workers.json ID Field Standardization
ideas.description: The dual-render bug (Luna appearing at 2 desks) is caused by workers.json entries missing the 'id' field. The Office component uses w.id to build activeIds Set for ghost filtering. Without id, activeIds contains 'undefined' and roster members aren't filtered. FIX: Ensure all workers.json entries include an 'id' field matching their roster id (lowercase name). WHY: This is a data contract bug that causes visual duplication of any working team member.

ideas.title: Mobile Desk Name Readability
ideas.description: On mobile (375px), desk nameplates are barely legible — the text is tiny and low contrast against the dark desk background. Bump mobile nameplate font-size to 0.38rem and add a subtle text-shadow for legibility. WHY: Mobile viewers can't tell who's at which desk without squinting.

ideas.title: Desk Hover Card with Worker Details
ideas.description: When hovering a desk (or tapping on mobile), show a mini-card with: worker name, role, current task, time on task. Currently you get a basic tooltip. A styled hover card would make the office feel interactive. WHY: Visitors want to know what each character is doing without switching to Dashboard.

ideas.title: On-Fire Floor Heat Shimmer
ideas.description: When data-vibe='on-fire', add a subtle upward-drifting orange shimmer via ::before pseudo-element on office-floor. Pure CSS keyframe animation. WHY: The on-fire vibe should feel different from the floor up, reinforcing the energy visually.

ideas.title: Office Tab Worker Count Badge
ideas.description: Add a small badge (e.g., '1' or '3/5') next to the Office tab button showing active worker count. WHY: Dashboard viewers can see team activity at a glance without switching tabs.

ideas.title: Daily Office Narrative Log
ideas.description: Auto-generate a short narrative paragraph from activity.json: 'Luna spent the morning reviewing designs while Carlos shipped 3 bug fixes...' Display in a collapsible section on Dashboard. WHY: Turns raw activity data into a story people want to read. Makes the office feel like it has history.
